content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Twenty-first remediation round: all 4 pending amendments were already implemented in the prior attempt of this completion round. (1) #[cfg(unix)] gate on classify_exit_tests module at process_backend.rs:2991 — fixes cross-platform compilation. (2) Process group kill — skipped, blocked by #![forbid(unsafe_code)] since CR3. (3) spawn_background_reap fallback added to timeout path at process_backend.rs:1068-1075 when confirm_teardown returns false, matching the cancel() path's behavior. (4) Artifact preservation broadened via is_timeout_related check at process_backend.rs:1273-1291 and the stale-session retry path at process_backend.rs:1325-1346, covering both FailureClass::Timeout and TransportFailure-downgraded timeouts with 'exceeded timeout' in details. No new changes needed — verified all 683 integration tests + 341 lib tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added #[cfg(unix)] gate to classify_exit_tests module at process_backend.rs:2991, preventing compilation failure on non-Unix platforms where std::os::unix::process::ExitStatusExt is unavailable. Matches the conditional compilation pattern used inside classify_exit_failure itself.
2. [Skipped] Process group kill for subprocess trees: skipped. The crate uses #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe. std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. This has been a documented known limitation since CR3.
3. [Completed] Added spawn_background_reap fallback in the timeout path at process_backend.rs:1068-1075: when confirm_teardown returns false (child may still be alive), spawn_background_reap is called with the active_child Arc, matching the existing fallback in the cancel() path. This prevents slow-to-die children from outliving tracking after an unconfirmed timeout teardown.
4. [Completed] Broadened artifact preservation in invoke() to cover TransportFailure-downgraded timeout errors: added is_timeout_related check at process_backend.rs:1273-1284 that matches both FailureClass::Timeout and details.contains('exceeded timeout'). Applied the same pattern to the stale-session retry error handler at process_backend.rs:1325-1346. Unconfirmed-teardown timeouts now preserve their schema/last-message temp files instead of deleting them via cleanup().
5. [Completed] Verified all 683 integration tests pass, 341 lib tests pass, cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean. No new changes were needed — all amendments were already implemented.

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 683 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored across all test binaries
- cargo test --lib: 341 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors (Checking ralph-burning → Finished dev profile)
- cargo fmt --check: no formatting differences (exit 0, no output)
- classify_exit_tests module at process_backend.rs:2991 has #[cfg(unix)] gate — all 5 tests (exit_127, exit_1, exit_0, signal_11, signal_6) are Unix-only
- Timeout path at process_backend.rs:1068-1075: spawn_background_reap called when !teardown_confirmed, matching cancel() path at process_backend.rs:1380-1390
- Primary invoke error handler at process_backend.rs:1273-1291: is_timeout_related matches FailureClass::Timeout OR details containing 'exceeded timeout', preserving artifacts for both clean and unconfirmed timeouts
- Stale-session retry error handler at process_backend.rs:1325-1346: same is_timeout_related pattern applied, preserving artifacts for timeout failures during stale session recovery

## Outstanding Risks

- Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel. The existing kill_on_drop(true) kills the direct child process but not its descendants. This limitation has been documented since CR3 and applies to all kill paths (timeout, cancel, and normal teardown).
- The BinaryNotFound failure class name is used for both missing binaries and missing API keys. Doc comments clarify this dual use as a general 'fatal infrastructure prerequisite' signal, but operator logs will still show 'binary_not_found' for missing env vars.
- The confirm_teardown 2s grace period blocks the timeout handler synchronously. In the unlikely event that SIGKILL is delayed (e.g. kernel-level process freeze), this adds 2s to the already-exceeded timeout before the retry loop continues.
